settings.title: Organization Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your organization, team members, and invitations
settings.defenseModeDescription: Control how the automated defense system responds to detected threats. This affects all alert rules.
settings.autoModeDesc: All rules execute automatically including blocking IPs, quarantining files, and sinkholing domains.
settings.semiAutoModeDesc: Rules detect threats and notify, but destructive actions (block, quarantine, sinkhole) require approval.
settings.manualModeDesc: Rules only generate notifications. All defensive actions must be manually executed by a user.
settings.simulatorDescription: Generate realistic security events to test your automated defense system. Events flow through the alert engine and trigger configured rules.

landing.typewriter1: Analyzing 2.5M+ threats per day across global networks...
landing.typewriter3: Mapping adversary TTPs to MITRE ATT&CK framework...
landing.heroDesc: Enterprise-grade AI-powered security operations center. Real-time threat detection, automated incident response, and comprehensive cyber defense for organizations that cannot afford to be breached.
landing.featuresDesc: Purpose-built security operations platform combining AI analysis with automated defense mechanisms
landing.featureAiDesc: Advanced machine learning models analyze security events in real-time, providing expert-level threat assessment and actionable recommendations.
landing.featureMonitorDesc: Live security event feeds via WebSocket connections, with instant alerting and customizable dashboards for complete situational awareness.
landing.featureDefenseDesc: Automated response playbooks execute countermeasures in milliseconds. IP blocking, asset isolation, file quarantine, and domain sinkholing.
landing.featureMitreDesc: Full tactical heatmap coverage across the MITRE ATT&CK framework. Track adversary techniques and measure detection capabilities.
landing.featureIncidentDesc: Structured incident management workflow from detection to resolution. Forensic timeline analysis and evidence preservation.
landing.featureIntelDesc: Aggregated threat intelligence feeds with IOC correlation. Automatic enrichment and threat scoring for proactive defense.
landing.ctaDesc: Deploy enterprise-grade cybersecurity for your organization. Full SOC capabilities with AI-powered threat detection and automated response.
landing.footerDesc: Enterprise-grade AI-powered cybersecurity platform providing comprehensive security operations center capabilities for businesses and organizations worldwide.

public.aboutMissionText: AegisAI360 was founded with a singular purpose: to provide enterprise-grade cybersecurity capabilities to organizations that protect critical infrastructure, business operations, and essential services. We believe that every organization deserves access to the same caliber of cyber defense used by the world's most secure institutions.
public.aboutValueProtectionDesc: Unwavering commitment to defending our clients' digital assets against the most sophisticated cyber threats.
public.aboutValueInnovationDesc: Continuous advancement of AI-powered defense mechanisms to stay ahead of evolving threat landscapes.
public.aboutValueExcellenceDesc: Delivering the highest standard of security operations with precision, reliability, and measurable outcomes.
public.aboutValueIntegrityDesc: Maintaining the highest ethical standards in data handling, threat disclosure, and client relationships.
public.aboutTeamDesc: Built by cybersecurity experts with decades of experience in enterprise cybersecurity. Our team combines deep technical expertise with operational security knowledge to deliver a platform that meets the demands of the most security-conscious organizations.
public.featuresSubtitle: Comprehensive security operations platform engineered for organizations that demand the highest level of cyber defense.
public.pricingSubtitle: Choose the level of protection that matches your operational requirements.
public.pricingStarterDesc: Essential security monitoring for small teams
public.pricingProDesc: Full SOC capabilities for growing security teams
public.pricingEnterpriseDesc: Unlimited security operations for large organizations
public.privacyIntro: AegisAI360 is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use, and safeguard your information.
public.privacyCollectText: We collect information you provide directly, such as account registration details, organization information, and security event data processed through our platform. We also collect usage data including log data, device information, and platform interaction metrics.
public.privacyUseText: Your information is used to provide and improve our security services, process and analyze security events, generate threat intelligence, send security alerts and notifications, and maintain platform security and integrity.
public.privacySecurityText: We implement enterprise-grade encryption and security measures to protect your data. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest. Access is strictly controlled through role-based permissions and multi-factor authentication.
public.privacyRetentionText: Security event data is retained according to your subscription plan. You may request deletion of your data at any time, subject to legal and regulatory requirements.
public.privacyThirdPartyText: We may share anonymized threat intelligence data with security partners to improve collective defense capabilities. We do not sell personal information to third parties.
public.privacyRightsText: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. You may also request data portability or restrict processing of your information.
public.privacyContactText: For privacy-related inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@aegisai360.com.
public.termsIntro: By accessing or using the AegisAI360 platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service.
public.termsAcceptText: By creating an account or using our services, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these terms and our Privacy Policy.
public.termsServiceText: AegisAI360 provides a cloud-based security operations center platform including threat monitoring, incident response, and security analytics capabilities. Service availability and features vary by subscription plan.
public.termsAccountsText: You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account credentials. You must notify us immediately of any unauthorized access. Each user must have a unique account.
public.termsUseText: You agree to use the platform only for lawful security monitoring purposes. You must not use the platform to conduct attacks, unauthorized scanning, or any activity that violates applicable laws.
public.termsIpText: All platform content, features, and functionality are owned by AegisAI360. Your security event data remains your property. You grant us a limited license to process this data for service delivery.
public.termsLiabilityText: AegisAI360 shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages. Our total liability is limited to the amount paid for the service in the preceding 12 months.
public.termsTerminationText: Either party may terminate the agreement with 30 days written notice. We may suspend service immediately for violations of these terms or non-payment.
public.termsLawText: These terms are governed by applicable law. Any disputes shall be resolved through binding arbitration.
public.termsContactText: For questions about these terms, contact legal@aegisai360.com.
public.refundIntro: We stand behind our platform and want you to be fully satisfied with your subscription.
public.refundEligibilityText: You may request a full refund within 14 days of your initial subscription purchase. Refunds are available for annual plans on a pro-rated basis within 30 days.
public.refundProcessText: To request a refund, contact our billing team at billing@aegisai360.com with your account details and reason for the request. We will process eligible refunds within 5-10 business days.
public.refundTimelineText: Approved refunds are processed within 5-10 business days. The refund will be credited to your original payment method.
public.refundExceptionsText: Refunds are not available for accounts that have been terminated for violations of our Terms of Service, or for partial months of service on monthly plans.

scanner.title: Security Scanner
scanner.subtitle: Scan targets for vulnerabilities, open ports, DNS records, SSL certificates, and security headers
scanner.subject: Subject
scanner.blockPortExplanation: Creates a firewall rule to block port {{port}}. This prevents external access through this port.
scanner.portRiskExplanation: Port {{port}} ({{service}}) is open and could be exploited
scanner.portRiskWhy: Open {{service}} ports can allow unauthorized access to your systems. Blocking unused ports reduces your attack surface.
scanner.blockPathExplanation: Creates a firewall rule and alert to block access to {{path}}. This protects sensitive files from being accessed.
scanner.vulnPathWhy: Exposed {{name}} can reveal sensitive configuration, credentials, or allow unauthorized access to admin tools.
scanner.monitorSSLExplanation: Creates an alert rule that will notify you when this SSL certificate is about to expire.
scanner.sslExpiredWhy: An expired certificate means your site is no longer secure. Visitors will see security warnings and their data could be intercepted.
scanner.sslExpiringExplanation: Your SSL certificate expires in {{days}} days
scanner.sslExpiringWhy: Your certificate is about to expire. Set up monitoring to be reminded before it goes down.
scanner.sslSelfSignedWhy: Self-signed certificates are not trusted by browsers. Visitors will see a security warning before accessing your site.
scanner.headerWhyImportant: This header provides {{description}} protection. Adding it to your server configuration will improve security.
scanner.subdomainWhatThisMeans: These are publicly accessible subdomains of your domain. Subdomains like admin, staging, or dev may expose sensitive services.
scanner.dirWhatThisMeans: These paths were found accessible on your server. Exposed admin panels, config files, or backup files can be exploited by attackers.
scanner.techWhatThisMeans: These technologies were detected running on the target. Knowing the tech stack helps identify version-specific vulnerabilities.
scanner.wafWhatThisMeans: A Web Application Firewall (WAF) protects the target from common attacks. This is generally a good security measure.
scanner.noWafDetected: No WAF detected. The target may be unprotected against common web attacks.
scanner.whoisWhatThisMeans: WHOIS data reveals domain ownership and registration details. This information is useful for investigating suspicious domains.
scanner.sqliWhatThisMeans: SQL injection allows attackers to manipulate database queries. This can lead to data theft, unauthorized access, or complete database compromise.
scanner.noSqliFound: No SQL injection vulnerabilities detected. Your application appears safe from basic SQLi attacks.
scanner.sqliRemediation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL queries.
scanner.xssWhatThisMeans: Cross-site scripting (XSS) allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users. This can steal cookies, session tokens, or redirect users.
scanner.noXssFound: No XSS vulnerabilities detected. Your application appears safe from basic reflected XSS attacks.
scanner.xssRemediation: Encode all user input before rendering in HTML. Use Content-Security-Policy headers to prevent inline script execution.

networkMonitor.title: Infrastructure Monitor
networkMonitor.subtitle: Monitor your servers, websites, and network endpoints with real security scans
networkMonitor.deviceBlocked: IP blocked and firewall rule created
networkMonitor.noDevices: No assets found. Add a server or website to start monitoring.

networkMonitor.noAssetsToScan: No assets to scan. Add a server or website first.

networkMonitor.noScanResults: No scan results yet. Click Re-scan to perform a scan.

hashTools.title: Hash & Password Tools
hashTools.subtitle: Identify hash types, attempt hash cracking, and analyze password strength
hashTools.hashIdentifierDesc: Paste a hash to identify its type
hashTools.noHashIdentified: Could not identify the hash type. Check that the value is a valid hash.
hashTools.hashCrackerDesc: Attempt to crack a hash using a dictionary attack
hashTools.notCrackedDesc: The hash was not found in the dictionary. The password may be strong or uncommon.
hashTools.hashCrackWhatThisMeans: Dictionary attacks try common passwords against the hash. If cracked, the password is weak and should be changed immediately.
hashTools.passwordAnalyzerDesc: Analyze password strength and get improvement suggestions
hashTools.couldBeCracked: This password could be cracked in {{time}}
hashTools.noResults: Enter a value and click the button to see results
